# Project Guidelines

## Project Overview

Playwright + TypeScript E2E test suite for [saucedemo.com](https://www.saucedemo.com).
Uses a layered fixture architecture and a strict Page Object Model (POM).

## Project Structure

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pages/` | Page objects (`BasePage` subclasses) |
| `pages/components/` | Reusable UI components (`BaseComponent` subclasses) |
| `tests/*.spec.ts` | Playwright spec files |
| `tests/fixture/` | Fixture infrastructure (types, worker, page, auth layers) |
| `tests-cases/` | Test case definitions in Markdown |
| `tests-setup/` | `global-setup.ts` for pre-test authentication |

## TypeScript Conventions

- Strict mode is enabled (`"strict": true`). Never use `any` or non-null assertion (`!`) without justification.
- Use `private readonly` for all page/component locator fields.
- Expose locators as getter properties only when tests need to assert on them directly.
- ES2022 target, CommonJS modules, `@playwright/test` types.

## Page Object Model

- **Pages** extend `BasePage` and receive a `Page` instance.
- **Components** extend `BaseComponent` and receive a scoped `Locator` (never `Page`).
- File naming: lowercase kebab-case, no class-name suffix.
  - ✅ `pages/login.ts`, `pages/components/cart-badge.ts`
  - ❌ `pages/LoginPage.ts`, `pages/components/CartBadgeComponent.ts`
- Locators use `data-test` attributes where available; fall back to stable IDs or roles.
- Never use raw `page.locator()` calls inside test files — all interactions go through page objects.

## Fixtures

- Tests import from `tests/fixture/test-fixtures.ts` (the stable entrypoint).
- Use `testNoAuth` for unauthenticated scenarios (e.g., login form tests).
- Use `test` for scenarios that require a pre-authenticated session.
- Worker-scoped storage state files live in `.auth/state-{index}.json` (generated by global-setup).
- Fixture types are declared in `tests/fixture/types.ts`; extend `TestFixtures` or `WorkerFixtures` there when adding new fixtures.

## Test Authoring

- Outer describe: `test.describe('Feature: <Feature Name>', () => { ... })`
- Test title format: `TC-NNN Short description` (e.g., `TC-001 Successful login with valid credentials`)
- Priority tag on every test: `{ tag: ['@high'] }` or `{ tag: ['@medium'] }` or `{ tag: ['@low'] }`
- Every `expect()` must include a descriptive failure message: `'TC-NNN: what should be true'`
- Skip a describe block instead of individual tests when a required env var is missing:
  ```ts
  test.skip(!getSauceDemoPassword(), 'Set SAUCE_DEMO_PASSWORD to run …');
  ```

## Secrets & Environment

- Credentials are sourced from environment variables via `tests/fixture/env.ts`.
- Use `getSauceDemoPassword()` for the shared test password. Never hardcode credentials.
- Required variable: `SAUCE_DEMO_PASSWORD`.

## Commands

```bash
npm test                  # Run all tests (headless)
npm run test:headed       # Run with visible browser
npm run test:ui           # Playwright UI mode
npm run auth              # Re-generate auth state files
```
